paper.title: Does inflation increase after a monetary policy tightening? Answers based on a estimated DSGE model
paper.journal_or_series: Journal of Economic Dynamics & Control 31(3), pp. 906-937

raw_pdf.source_role: manually downloaded source PDF; body not opened by default

appendix_normalization.source_role: hand-normalized appendix formula inventory when present

implementation_cross_check.source_role: MMB implementation cross-check only; not paper-side mathematical source
implementation_cross_check.notes: Used to confirm model(linear), active variable list, shock names, and the added labor-notation identity n=l. Not used to source mathematical formulas.

source_sniff.expected_title: Does inflation increase after a monetary policy tightening? Answers based on a estimated DSGE model
source_sniff.observed_title: Does inflation increase after a monetary policy tightening? Answers based on an estimated DSGE model
source_sniff.notes: Only the article in the title differs between the model index and first-page OCR/published title: 'a estimated' versus 'an estimated'.

spans: first-page title, author, abstract, bibliographic metadata
Section 2 model primitives: production, labor aggregation, final-good aggregation, household problem, capital accumulation, price and wage setting, policy and resource constraint
Sections 4-6 and Tables 1-2 for calibration, priors, estimated baseline values, and interpretation of cost-channel restrictions

notes: The paper's linearized model equations are legible in MinerU Markdown, but several OCR artifacts remain in nonlinear primitives and notation.
No PDF-body formula check was performed.
The implementation adds n=l for notation consistency; this is recorded only as implementation_cross_check.

runtime_validation.notes: Current task is private derivation extraction only; Dynare was explicitly not run.

copyright_boundary: The derivation summarizes model structure and equations. It does not copy long prose from the paper.
